research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
finding: High EHR alert load was linked with clinicians reporting missed abnormal results.
what_happened: PCPs received many alerts and reported overload, poor usability, and handoff problems.
what_was_identified: Need for action on abnormal results was identified as a safety-critical workflow.
what_was_missed: Abnormal results could be missed or delayed.
cause_or_contributing_factor: information overload; EHR usability; electronic handoffs
outcome_or_consequence: delayed care reported by some clinicians
product_or_system_implication: Risk-ranked result inbox with owner, severity, patient-notified status, and escalation.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
stage_or_process_step: first assessment / history / exam / tests / referral
finding: Reviewed diagnostic-error cases showed breakdowns across history, exam, testing, referral, and tracking.
what_happened: 190 diagnostic-error cases were reviewed using EHR-based triggers.
what_was_identified: Multiple process breakdown points were identified.
what_was_missed: Common diseases could be missed, including pneumonia, heart failure, renal failure, cancer, UTI/pyelonephritis.
outcome_or_consequence: moderate to severe harm potential in many cases
product_or_system_implication: Return-visit flags, diagnostic checklist, and tracking for common high-harm misses.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
condition_or_issue: complex patients and limited English proficiency
finding: Clinicians often needed more time than allotted.
what_happened: Time pressure was especially high with complex psychosocial needs and limited English proficiency.
what_was_identified: Time mismatch was identified.
what_was_missed: Subtle red flags or context could be missed.
outcome_or_consequence: incomplete history and less thorough synthesis risk
product_or_system_implication: Previsit narrative capture and interpreter/social-complexity preparation.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
finding: Safety-netting plans varied in specificity and responsibility.
what_happened: GPs used monitoring, treatment, and administrative risk strategies.
what_was_identified: Uncertainty-management strategies were identified.
what_was_missed: Worsening symptoms may not be acted on without a clear plan.
outcome_or_consequence: delayed re-presentation or missed escalation risk
product_or_system_implication: Safety-net plan with red flags, timescale, responsible clinician, and follow-up trigger.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
person_or_group_involved: patient, ED, GP, physiotherapist, nurse, out-of-hours GPs
condition_or_issue: severe back pain after prior breast cancer
finding: Multiple contacts over eight months preceded diagnosis of spinal metastatic cancer.
what_happened: Back pain was treated and later investigated after spinal lump was found.
what_was_identified: Spinal metastatic cancer was ultimately identified.
what_was_missed: Repeat unresolving symptom and prior cancer history were not connected early.
cause_or_contributing_factor: fragmented care; repeated visits; poor continuity
product_or_system_implication: Unresolved-symptom timeline and prior cancer/history surfacing.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
condition_or_issue: worsening skin lesion with prior skin cancer
finding: A second online request lacked prior cancer history and was triaged as routine.
what_happened: Later GP review generated an urgent referral.
what_was_identified: Worsening skin symptoms and prior cancer were ultimately considered.
what_was_missed: Prior cancer history and deterioration were not joined in the online triage step.
cause_or_contributing_factor: context loss in online forms; admin triage
outcome_or_consequence: delayed urgent suspicion after second request
product_or_system_implication: Online intake should auto-surface major history and related recent contacts.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
person_or_group_involved: patients, admin staff, GPs, software developers
finding: Online forms can lose nuance when they force single-symptom routes or depend on free text.
what_happened: HSSIB described risks in both structured and free-text intake.
what_was_identified: Need for safer intake design was identified.
what_was_missed: Complexity and patient meaning may be missed.
product_or_system_implication: Hybrid intake: narrative, adaptive red flags, prior-history auto-pull, complexity escape hatch.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
condition_or_issue: stroke, MI, aortic dissection, spinal cord compression, VTE, meningitis, sepsis, etc.
finding: Serious ED diagnostic harms concentrated in a set of high-risk conditions.
what_happened: A systematic review identified high-risk conditions and presentation patterns.
what_was_identified: Atypical/subtle presentation problem was identified.
what_was_missed: High-risk disease behind common symptoms can be missed.
outcome_or_consequence: serious harm, death, or permanent disability risk
product_or_system_implication: High-risk diagnosis overlays by symptom, repeat visit, vitals, and risk factors.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
finding: Commentary argued ED misdiagnosis should be understood in the broader system of crowding and safety risk.
what_happened: AHRQ ED diagnostic-error framing was critiqued.
what_was_identified: Crowding/systemic risk was identified.
what_was_missed: Diagnostic attention and follow-up may degrade under crowding.
product_or_system_implication: Crowding-aware diagnostic review triggers and workload dashboards.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
finding: Triage nurses reported pressure, cognitive strain, and emotional strain during acuity decisions.
what_happened: Interviews identified training, experience, culture, and strain as decision factors.
what_was_identified: Need for training/support was identified.
what_was_missed: High-risk patients may be under-triaged.
product_or_system_implication: Triage co-pilot with red flags, reassessment timers, and escalation support.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
finding: Staff fatigue contributes directly and indirectly to patient harm but is not routinely captured.
what_happened: HSSIB gathered staff accounts and safety-system findings.
what_was_identified: Fatigue as system risk was identified.
what_was_missed: Attentional lapses, communication problems, or task omissions can be missed.
cause_or_contributing_factor: long shifts; staffing pressure; inadequate fatigue governance
outcome_or_consequence: patient harm and staff retention risk
product_or_system_implication: Fatigue-aware task routing and second-check triggers for high-risk diagnostic tasks.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
setting_or_context: referral hospitals: ED, ICU, wards, maternity
finding: Poor handover communication was linked to documentation, training, procedure, and culture barriers.
what_happened: Qualitative interviews described communication barriers during handovers.
what_was_identified: Handover barriers were identified.
what_was_missed: Diagnosis, medications, treatment changes, and pending tasks may be missed.
cause_or_contributing_factor: poor documentation; lack of formal procedure; interpersonal barriers
product_or_system_implication: Standardized handoff object with diagnosis, uncertainty, pending tests, watch-outs, and next owner.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
finding: Communication gaps remained despite EHR diffusion.
what_happened: Survey identified persistent PCP-specialist communication gaps.
what_was_identified: Need for better communication was identified.
what_was_missed: Specialist feedback or PCP clinical question may not connect.
outcome_or_consequence: missed follow-up or unclear next step
product_or_system_implication: Referral object with clinical question, urgency, attachments, consultant response, next owner.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
finding: Closed diagnostic referral loops remain a safety problem.
what_happened: Systems-engineering analysis described failures and redesign approaches.
what_was_identified: Referral-loop failure points were identified.
what_was_missed: Specialist evaluation or test may be missed.
cause_or_contributing_factor: unclear ownership; scheduling and feedback gaps
product_or_system_implication: Closed-loop referral tracker with due dates and exception handling.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
finding: Enhanced usability and longitudinal tracking lowered cognitive workload and improved abnormal-result management.
what_happened: Simulation compared EHR designs.
what_was_identified: Usability and longitudinal tracking benefits were identified.
what_was_missed: Abnormal results can be lost in poor interfaces.
product_or_system_implication: Longitudinal test-trend view and task-state tracking.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
finding: Long and redundant notes can obscure important information.
what_happened: Review/commentary described note growth and redundancy.
what_was_identified: Documentation burden and obscured information were identified.
what_was_missed: New facts, changed assessment, and unresolved problems may be hidden.
product_or_system_implication: Note-diff, copied-forward detection, concise problem timeline.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
finding: Difficult patient behavior negatively affected diagnostic performance in experiments.
what_happened: Physicians reviewed vignettes with disruptive behaviors.
what_was_identified: Effect of patient behavior on reasoning was identified.
what_was_missed: Correct diagnosis behind behavior could be missed.
product_or_system_implication: Bias-aware diagnostic pause when stigmatizing labels or difficult interactions appear.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
person_or_group_involved: ED doctors, nurses, psychiatric liaison teams
condition_or_issue: physical symptoms in patients with mental illness
finding: Physical symptoms may be misattributed to mental illness.
what_happened: Qualitative study explored diagnostic overshadowing and ED challenges.
what_was_identified: Diagnostic overshadowing risk was identified.
what_was_missed: Physical illness can be missed when symptoms are attributed to psychiatric causes.
product_or_system_implication: Medical red-flag checklist for mental-health presentations and vitals/baseline trend display.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
finding: CHWs reported barriers to early cancer detection and referral.
what_happened: Qualitative study identified training, infrastructure, referral, fear, finance, and sociocultural barriers.
what_was_identified: Community-level barriers were identified.
what_was_missed: Early cancer signs and referral completion may be missed.
cause_or_contributing_factor: limited training; infrastructure; referral barriers; sociocultural beliefs
product_or_system_implication: CHW decision support, local-language education, referral navigation, follow-up queue.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
person_or_group_involved: primary care clinicians, labs, health systems
stage_or_process_step: labs ordered / imaging ordered / public screening
finding: Large global diagnostic-access gap limits confirmation and monitoring.
what_happened: Lancet Commission summary documented inadequate access to diagnostics.
what_was_identified: Access gap was identified.
what_was_missed: Conditions may remain unconfirmed or unmonitored.
outcome_or_consequence: delayed or absent diagnosis in low-resource contexts
product_or_system_implication: Resource-aware workflows, point-of-care test inventory, offline referral escalation.

research_topic: Research D — clinician-side pain points in identifying health issues early
finding: Clinician commentary emphasized observing the actual clinician screen and workflow.
what_happened: Podcast/blog commentary discussed diagnostic-error reduction in ED.
what_was_identified: Need for workflow-grounded design was identified.
what_was_missed: Prior history, pending tests, and red flags may be scattered.
product_or_system_implication: Field observation and screen-level workflow mapping before designing tools.
